note: Mathlib/Analysis/Asymptotics/SpecificAsymptotics.lean:189 - proves only the Cesaro-mean corollary (b_n=n, arithmetic mean of a convergent sequence tends to the same limit), not the general Stolz-Cesaro ratio theorem for arbitrary strictly-monotone unbounded b_n; see also cesaro_smul at line 174.
verify_note: Decl confirmed at cited line; source shows Tendsto.cesaro is literally b_n=n via .cesaro_smul, Wikidata confirms Q1052752 is the general ratio theorem (Cesaro mean is only its corollary) — special_case is the honest, non-overclaimed relation.

note: Mathlib/Combinatorics/SimpleGraph/Coloring/EdgeLabeling.lean:37 - docstring explicitly disclaims equivalence: 'Sometimes this is called an edge-coloring, but we reserve that terminology for labelings where incident edges cannot share a label' - no proper-edge-coloring predicate (adjacent edges distinct) exists in Mathlib.
verify_note: Decl confirmed at cited line; docstring quote verified verbatim, repo-wide grep found no properEdgeColoring predicate, Wikidata description of Q1050972 requires the properness constraint EdgeLabeling explicitly lacks — related/low is correctly conservative, not overclaimed.

note: Mathlib/Data/Nat/Digits/Defs.lean:78 - generic base-b positional digit representation `digits : ℕ → ℕ → List ℕ`; ternary is the b=3 instance, no dedicated ternary-numeral-system decl exists.
verify_note: Decl confirmed at cited line (`def digits : ℕ → ℕ → List ℕ`); Wikidata Q1056486 confirms concept is base-3 numeral system, a special case of the generic base-b digits function; grep for 'ternary' in Mathlib turns up only an unrelated Cantor-set-specific stream, so generalization/medium is honest.
